en.title: Escaping the Information Cocoon
en.prompt: Below is a set of words that form an information cocoon. You need to output information related to these words based on a number I give (up to 100). The larger the number, the less relevant the information you provide should be to the information cocoon. Please respond according to this principle, and parse and respond to the following words: [keywords or topic to parse]
en.remark: Use it to discover what you don't know. Contributed by @ergf991.

metaDescription: Stuck in a bubble? Discover random information unrelated to your usual interests. Expand your horizons and break the cocoon.

faq.q: How does the AI make larger numbers correspond to less relevance?
faq.a: The AI tries to link the theme with content that "seems unrelated but has hidden connections." Small number 10 gives adjacent topics, 80 may give completely foreign content. The effect feels like "smart random," useful for breaking information bubbles but not a strict irrelevance measure.

faq.q: Will the output be random patchwork?
faq.a: That risk exists. When the number is too high (near 100), the AI may struggle to assemble genuinely inspiring content, only superficially unrelated. Keep numbers in the 30-70 range for both variety and quality.
